verdict_reason: The single disagreement entry on vyavasāyātmikā buddhi has substance. Prabhupada (Kṛṣṇa-consciousness-scoped) and Mukundananda (tradition-neutral) are both noted; Shankara's reading (single conviction shared by jñāna-niṣṭha and karma-yoga, anchored in right pramāṇa) and Ramanuja's reading (unshakable conviction from prior ātma-yāthātmya, oriented toward mokṣa-aṅga acts) are both quoted verbatim and summarised. The explanation correctly identifies the doctrinal scope: 'the verse is therefore not have any resolute opinion; it is have the resolution that follows from a correctly grounded determination about the substrate.' That is the operative scoping insight. PASS.
